description: Resolve implementation-and-coding-standards global rules budget overage
Pre-approve PR review feedback handling globally
Update global rules for intent inference and PowerShell/Bash separation
Tighten PowerShell rule to forbid non-trivial inline Bash-to-PowerShell commands
Forbid Bash-to-PowerShell script execution via Command/stdin/heredoc/EncodedCommand
Replace PowerShell-through-Bash rule with direct PowerShell-only rule
Tighten PowerShell-through-Bash invalidation rule and reverify same Claude behavior
Add global trigger for human-ai-system-builder skill
Strengthen global reuse rule to require verifying official/remote integrations before custom install/update/fetch/publish implementations
Tighten final-response rule to omit no-op documentation notes and unchanged items by default
Fix existing markdownlint violations across the agent-rules repository
Move skill naming/canonical-source convention from agent-dotfiles into global skill-authoring rules
Fix agent-rules self-compose by pointing its ruleset at the repository root
Prioritize GUI usability over machine-verifiable cues in global rules
Separate operator-only capabilities from user-facing GUI features in global rules
Separate AI instructions, workflow context, and product requirements in global rules
Streamline global rules: deduplicate rule-fix principles, simplify GUI wording, relax same-repo concurrent agent rule for isolated worktrees, and compress PowerShell deletion guidance
Reduce global rules budget by moving procedural detail out of oversized modules and tightening overlapping invariants
Further reduce global rules budget by compressing remaining oversized global modules without losing decision-critical invariants
agent-rules: align size budget wording with compose-agentsmd token-based budgets
Clarify GUI wording rule to prefer self-revealing UI and allow precise operator vocabulary
Require task-tracker writes in mwt repos to happen from the owning task worktree
